rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOBROCKE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Bayer Cropscience, Inc.¿s motion to compel production of documents is granted and its request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs must provide the responsive documents (and if desired by them with redactions for claimed work product and a privilege log) either in electronic or hard copy form without cost to defendant. Because the redactions could be done electronically, plaintiffs¿ request for 15 cents per page is essentially a request to be paid for the time and effort spent doing the redactions. This is not a recoverable expense. (See San Diego Unified Port Dist. v. Douglas E. Barnhart, Inc. (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 1400, 1404 (¿[P]arties are expected to bear expenses typically involved in responding to discovery requests, such as the expense of producing documents.¿)). Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied because the issue raised by the motion had previously been unsettled. = (220/HEK)
